sourceMovie.title: Thoroughly Modern Millie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Thoroughly Modern Millie works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and music blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Coyote Ugly
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thoroughly Modern Millie clicked for you, Coyote Ugly (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and music, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thoroughly Modern Millie?
faq.answer: Try Coyote Ugly, The Lobster, Hitch—each shares DNA with Thoroughly Modern Millie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thoroughly Modern Millie?
faq.answer: Thoroughly Modern Millie (1967) is best described as Comedy, Music, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thoroughly Modern Millie worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
